paper.title_en: Stochastic Evolution of Primordial Black Holes to near-extremality in EFTs of Gravity
paper.abstract_en: The search for dark matter candidates includes primordial black holes (PBHs) as possible constituents. Recent studies show that some PBHs can survive to the present epoch by gaining angular momentum through Hawking radiating photons and becoming extremal before complete evaporation. While this provides a plausible model in a two-derivative theory of gravity, additional issues arise in EFT-corrected theories of gravity. In such theories, a rapidly spinning black hole can lead to extremely high tidal forces on a near-horizon observer, with possible observational consequences. In this work, by modeling Hawking radiation as a biased random walk within an EFT of gravity, we show that nearly the same fraction of PBHs survives as in GR. We argue that the resultant near horizon tidal effects should be detectable in future gravitational-wave observables.

decision.selection_reason_zh: 研究原初黑洞在有效场论框架下的随机演化，涉及霍金辐射和极端性，理论深度高，方法严谨，具有新颖性，与黑洞热力学和引力基础高度相关。
decision.title_zh: 原初黑洞在引力有效场论中向近极端性的随机演化
decision.abstract_zh: 寻找暗物质候选者的研究包括原初黑洞作为可能的组成部分。近年来的研究表明，一些原初黑洞通过霍金辐射光子获得角动量，并在完全蒸发前变为极端黑洞，从而可能存活至今。虽然这在二导数引力理论中提供了一个合理的模型，但在引力有效场论修正的理论中会出现额外的问题。在这样的理论中，快速旋转的黑洞可能导致近地平观测者承受极高的潮汐力，并可能产生可观测的后果。在这项工作中，通过将霍金辐射建模为引力有效场论中的有偏随机游走，我们展示了几乎相同比例的原初黑洞存活下来，与广义相对论中一致。我们认为，由此产生的近地平潮汐效应应在未来的引力波可观测信号中可探测到。
